surface.name: Send and verify a first Mistral API request with Python
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Send and verify a first Mistral API request with Python. The official developer quickstart defines a roughly five-minute Python route and a printed model response as its Verify state. Prior surface before policy commit: Unresolved among Vibe, Studio, and Developer API quickstarts.

alternatives_considered.name: Run your first Vibe Work task
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the first Vibe Work quickstart, but Vibe is only one of three primary products.

alternatives_considered.name: Install the Vibe CLI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a coding-agent route, not a universal Mistral platform path.

alternatives_considered.name: Activate Studio and generate an API key
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the first Studio setup route and ends with a working key, but the docs do not prefer Studio over Vibe or Admin for all visitors.

alternatives_considered.name: Send your first API request
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the strongest API-developer route and explicitly ends in a printed model response, but it is one product/role filter among equal quickstarts.

alternatives_considered.name: Create your organization
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the Admin route for IT, security, and finance, not a general individual developer route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Choose which Mistral product and first outcome the workshop means before reconstructing its complete first mile.
assumed_prior_state: No product-specific intent has been supplied for Mistral AI.
The researcher must not infer that API use, Vibe use, or organization administration is the universal first success.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The selected first-API-request route demonstrates first success when the terminal prints the model's short description of Mistral AI rather than an authentication, billing, or rate-limit error.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The terminal contains the short description rather than an authentication, billing, or rate-limit error.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The terminal contains the short description rather than an authentication, billing, or rate-limit error.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The terminal contains the short description rather than an authentication, billing, or rate-limit error.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A declared product and developer intent: Vibe Work, Vibe Code, Studio, Developer API, or Admin.

details: The page presents Vibe, Studio, and Admin and a filterable collection of product/role quickstarts.
primary_path.input: Intent to start using Mistral AI.
primary_path.success_signal: The Discover our platform and Quickstarts sections are visible.

primary_path.action: Choose the product and first-use outcome before continuing.
details: Studio covers the developer console, keys, Playground, agents, evaluations, and API SDKs.
Admin covers organization, billing, SSO, workspaces, and policies.
primary_path.success_signal: A product-specific guide with its own Verify or terminal state is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The supplied roster entry does not identify this choice, so research stops rather than inferring one.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to a Mistral account and confirm that Python 3.9 or later is available.
details: The quickstart lists a Mistral account and Python 3.9 or later as prerequisites.
Free mode is enabled by default and does not require a credit card.
primary_path.input: A usable email identity and a local Python 3.9 or later environment.
primary_path.output: An authenticated account and compatible runtime.
primary_path.success_signal: Mistral Studio is accessible and the Python version satisfies the documented prerequisite.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account signup UI details and timing are not decomposed in the quickstart; a 402 later means the selected usage requires a payment method.

primary_path.action: Open Studio, go to API keys, and select Create new key.
details: API keys are created from the Studio API keys page.
primary_path.success_signal: The form asks for a key name.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The page is unavailable if Studio access has not been established.

primary_path.action: Name the key, create it, and copy the secret immediately.
details: The secret is displayed only once.
primary_path.output: A newly generated Mistral API key secret.
primary_path.success_signal: The key is shown and copied to secure temporary storage.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the one-time secret is not copied, create another key.

primary_path.action: Set the copied secret as the MISTRAL_API_KEY environment variable.
details: The sample reads the secret from the environment instead of embedding it in source code.
primary_path.success_signal: The environment variable is present in the shell used to run the sample.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A later 401 indicates that the key is incorrect or not set; the guide recommends checking it with echo $MISTRAL_API_KEY.

primary_path.action: Install the Mistral Python SDK with pip install mistralai.
details: This installs the package imported by the documented sample.
primary_path.output: The mistralai package in the active Python environment.
primary_path.success_signal: The package installation completes without an error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package or environment errors must be resolved before the sample can import Mistral.

primary_path.action: Create quickstart.py with the documented Mistral client and chat completion code.
details: Create a Mistral client, call client.chat.complete with mistral-large-latest, ask What is Mistral AI?, and print the response content.
primary_path.input: The official Python sample and configured environment variable.
primary_path.success_signal: The file contains the client initialization, request, and print statement.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Syntax, import, or environment-variable mistakes prevent execution.

details: The script sends the first chat-completion request to the Mistral API.
primary_path.input: The runnable sample, SDK, API key, and network access.
primary_path.output: An API response or an HTTP error.
primary_path.success_signal: The command completes and prints response text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: 401 means the key is wrong or unset; 402 means a payment method is required for the attempted usage; 429 means the rate limit was reached and the guide says to wait and use exponential backoff.

primary_path.action: Verify that the terminal prints a short description of Mistral AI.
details: This printed model response is the quickstart's explicit Verify state.
primary_path.output: A human-readable model response describing Mistral AI.
primary_path.success_signal: The terminal contains the short description rather than an authentication, billing, or rate-limit error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No response or an error means first success has not been reached.

branches.condition: The intended outcome is a first API response from code.
branches.path: Follow Send your first API request: ensure Python 3.9+ or Node.js 18+, create and store a Studio key, set MISTRAL_API_KEY, install the SDK, create the sample file, run it, and verify a short description prints.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines the boundary as a printed model response and carries the vendor's approximately five-minute claim.

branches.condition: The intended outcome is a working Studio key.
branches.path: Follow Activate Studio and generate an API key: enter API Keys, create and configure a key, copy it once, store it securely, and optionally test it with curl.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Stops before SDK installation at a securely stored key or successful key-test response.

branches.condition: The intended outcome is no-code model evaluation.
branches.path: Follow Test a model in the API playground: open Playground, select a model, send a prompt, vary parameters, compare outputs, and complete Verify.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses the Studio console and model responses instead of local code.

branches.condition: The intended outcome is Vibe or organization administration.
branches.path: Select the corresponding Vibe Work, Vibe Code, or Admin quickstart and research that guide independently.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the prerequisites, interfaces, actions, and terminal signal entirely.

friction_gates.description: The documentation requires a product and role choice before a first-mile path exists.
Every inspected candidate requires a Mistral account or product access, but the exact access path differs.
The Developer API candidate requires a one-time displayed API key stored securely.
Studio Free mode is documented as enabled by default with no credit card, while organization/Admin paths can introduce billing setup.

sources.title: Platform overview
evidence_supported: Explicit instruction to pick a product
Different roles and outcomes by product

sources.title: Activate Studio and generate an API key
Test a model in the API playground
sections_used: Step 2: Select a model and send a prompt
Step 3: Adjust parameters and compare outputs

uncertainties.question: Which Mistral product and role should the workshop record measure?
uncertainties.impact: It changes the first-success boundary and the complete journey.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The roster names the company only, while official docs explicitly require a product choice.

uncertainties.question: If Developer API is chosen, should success stop at securely generating a key or at printing the first model response?
uncertainties.impact: These are separate official quickstarts with different terminal outcomes.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Both are first-use quickstarts and the API guide includes the key guide as a prerequisite path.

excluded_after_success: No downstream work is excluded until a product-specific first-success boundary is chosen.
